batch_summary.note: Sep-Oct 2024. Highest RF-14 flag density in any single batch: 4 primary flags. S-008: Bike safety gear dispute (Sep 6). S-009: Eli discovers and fixes unsafe skateboard trucks (Sep 8). S-010: Choking/arm-twisting harm relay via Olivia (Sep 15). S-011: Melatonin 'drugging' accusation (Oct 3). Separate context: doctor visit (Oct 1 bee sting urgent care) noted normal bumps as molluscum contagiosum — not bruising. ARC-RF14-001 WATCHING→ACTIVE (7+ signals). ARC-RF14-002 ACTIVE continuing.

primary_flags.description: ROW-9266: 'She has never ridden a bike without training wheels. Common sense is to ensure she has proper safety gear in case she falls.' ROW-9280: 'Because at the beginning there is greater risk of her falling and causing injury.' ROW-9292: 'If she would have gotten hurt you would have felt bad. She would have had unnecessary pain.' ROW-9293: 'I don't want her hurt and you don't either. Take the precautions.' Melissa issuing safety directives about bike riding without safety gear. Legitimate parenting concern but delivered as allegation of unsafe parenting by Eli.

ROW-9322: 'Was the trucks on her skateboard loosened or did someone loosen them? That loose makes it very unsafe. I tightened them.' Eli discovering Olivia's skateboard has dangerously loose trucks, suspects tampering, and immediately fixes them. This is a genuine proactive safety observation — Eli testing equipment and identifying a safety hazard. ROW-9369: 'I was testing it and good thing I did she could have got hurt.' Appropriate protective parenting behavior.

ROW-9455: 'She said you tickle too hard and she doesn't like it.' ROW-9456: 'She said that you kept twisting her arm and it hurt and you'd say sorry but kept doing it.' ROW-9457: 'And she said that you choked her and she couldn't breathe for a bit.' ROW-9458: 'She said you were just playing but you play too rough and she doesn't like it.' ROW-9461: 'You used to do that shit with me and tell me you weren't hurting me. Don't do that to her.' Note: Olivia herself characterized it as rough play (ROW-9458). Melissa escalates to 'choked.' Personal history invoked. Harm relay via Olivia.
primary_flags.rf15_compound: RF-15 Category A peripheral — harm relay allegation with personal history invocation

primary_flags.description: ROW-9768: 'You gave her melatonin to get her to sleep instead of just being with her to comfort her. Your first choice was to drug her.' Melissa characterizing melatonin administration as 'drugging.' Melatonin is an OTC sleep supplement commonly used for children. Context: Oct 3 doctor visit for bee stings (urgent care Oct 1); doctor noted molluscum contagiosum bumps (Olivia's ongoing skin condition), not bruising. Melissa's 'drugging' framing is a substance weaponization of routine pediatric sleep aid.
primary_flags.rf15_compound: RF-15 peripheral — medication weaponization false allegation

peripheral_flags.note: Harm relay — choking/arm-twisting, personal history invoked. RF-15 primary for relay mechanism.
Melatonin 'drugging' accusation — medication weaponization. RF-15 peripheral.

ROW-9659: After bee sting urgent care, Eli reports 'the DR asked about her other bumps and bruises and did ask who she stayed with and then made some notes.' Doctor notation of bumps/bruises and custody context — medical provider documenting. Explained as molluscum contagiosum per Melissa (ROW-9663-9693). Positive documentation of medical care.

arc_updates.event: RF14-S-008 + S-010 + S-011: bike safety dispute + choking relay + melatonin accusation. Signal count now 8. Pattern fully established — pattern of manufactured safety allegations is consistent, recurrent, and escalating. ARC-RF14-001 promoted WATCHING→ACTIVE.
RF14-S-009: Skateboard trucks safety observation. Signal count now 4. ACTIVE maintained — genuine safety documentation pattern confirmed.
